source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original Free-Mission UAV brief. Wing-spar + carry-through remain the primary load path; surrounding airframe bodies (fuselage shells, tail empennage, landing-gear struts, motor mount, payload hatch) are added so the assembled FEM can accept lift-distribution, landing, and wingtip gust loads at their physical application points. No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Deliver the primary airframe assembly of a fixed-wing UAV competing in the national aerospace student benchmark Uluslararası Serbest Görev İHA (International Free-Mission UAV) category, modelled as a multi-body assembly rather than a single monolithic body. MTOW including 2.0 kg mission payload must not exceed 25 kg. The wing is a constant-chord rectangular planform with span 3.2 m, chord 0.35 m, NACA 4415 airfoil; cruise speed 22 m/s at 1500 m altitude (ISA rho = 1.058 kg/m^3). The primary wing-spar is a rectangular hollow box running the full span, joined at the root to a carbon-fiber carry-through that bolts into the fuselage frame via a 4-bolt pattern using M6 A4-70 stainless bolts. The spar and carry-through are T700/epoxy UD CFRP (E_long 130 GPa, E_trans 8 GPa, G_12 4.5 GPa, Xt 2150 MPa, Xc 1200 MPa, ILSS 70 MPa, rho 1550 kg/m^3). Limit load factor is +4 g / -2 g per STANAG 4703 light-UAS airworthiness; ultimate safety factor 1.5. Load cases (unchanged): (LC1) +4 g pull-up with elliptical lift across the full span on 25 kg MTOW (total 981 N at quarter-chord); (LC2) -2 g push-over with inverted elliptical lift (total 490 N downward); (LC3) asymmetric roll with 2.5 g on one half-span and 1.0 g on the other (combined unbalanced 428 N); (LC4) hard-landing 3 g vertical at main-landing-gear root on the fuselage frame with wings in static 1 g trim; (LC5) ground-gust 250 N drag on wingtip of parked aircraft. Pass/fail criteria (unchanged): (R1) Tsai-Wu composite failure index < 1.0 at ultimate = 1.5 x limit on every load case; (R2) wingtip deflection at 1 g steady flight < 2% of half-span (32 mm); (R3) first wing-bending natural frequency > 8 Hz; (R4) wing-spar + carry-through dry mass <= 2.2 kg; (R5) bolt-bearing stress at carry-through < 350 MPa at ultimate. In this multi-part rendering the airframe is decomposed into ten design-specific bodies: (A) wing main spar (1), (B) wing ribs distributed across the span (8), (C) wing-root carry-through block (1), (D) fuselage forward shell (1), (E) fuselage aft shell (1), (F) tail boom (1), (G) horizontal stabilizer (1), (H) vertical stabilizer (1), (I) landing-gear struts (2), (J) motor mount plate (1), (K) payload hatch (1), (L) carry-through fastener set (4 x M6 A4-70 bolts). Inter-part connections: wing spar to carry-through block (bonded + bolted at root fitting), ribs bonded along spar, carry-through block bolted to fuselage forward-shell frame, fuselage forward/aft shells bolted to bulkhead flange, tail boom clamped into aft shell, horizontal and vertical stabilizers screwed to tail boom, landing-gear struts bolted to fuselage forward shell, motor mount plate bolted to forward shell nose ring, payload hatch bolted to payload bay frame. Final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED airframe; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criterion R_asm1 is added. Deliverables: STEP AP242 multi-body model with one body per part family plus one body per rib and bolt instance, composite layup schedule for A/B/C, FEA report with Tsai-Wu index per ultimate load case, modal report for R3, mass table, assembly-deflection report at the wing-to-fuselage interface, per-connection DCR table.
short_prompt:
Deliver the primary airframe assembly of a fixed-wing UAV competing in the national aerospace student benchmark Uluslararası Serbest Görev İHA (International Free-Mission UAV) category, modelled as a multi-body assembly.

Envelope: MTOW including 2.0 kg mission payload <= 25 kg. Constant-chord rectangular wing, span 3.2 m, chord 0.35 m, NACA 4415 airfoil; cruise 22 m/s at 1500 m (ISA rho = 1.058 kg/m^3). Carry-through bolts into fuselage via 4-bolt M6 A4-70 pattern.

Material: T700/epoxy UD CFRP (Xt 2150 MPa, Xc 1200 MPa, ILSS 70 MPa). Limit load factor +4 g / -2 g per STANAG 4703 light-UAS airworthiness; ultimate safety factor 1.5.

Load cases: (LC1) +4 g pull-up elliptical lift full span on 25 kg (total 981 N at quarter-chord); (LC2) -2 g push-over inverted elliptical (total 490 N down); (LC3) asymmetric roll 2.5 g one half-span, 1.0 g other (unbalanced 428 N); (LC4) hard-landing 3 g vertical at main-gear root, wings at 1 g trim; (LC5) ground-gust 250 N drag on wingtip.

Pass/fail: (R1) Tsai-Wu < 1.0 at ultimate on every LC; (R2) wingtip deflection at 1 g < 32 mm (2% half-span); (R3) first wing-bending > 8 Hz; (R4) spar + carry-through dry mass <= 2.2 kg; (R5) bolt-bearing at carry-through < 350 MPa at ultimate.

prompt.title: Free-Mission UAV Airframe Assembly: Wing Spar + Carry-Through in Context (multi-part)
prompt.brief: 25 kg MTOW Free-Mission UAV airframe assembly centred on the CFRP wing-spar + carry-through primary load path, with ribs, fuselage shells, tail boom, empennage, landing-gear struts, motor mount, and payload hatch as separate bodies. +4/-2 g STANAG 4703 gates preserved; FEM verified on assembled airframe, connection DCR <= 1.0.

material.name: T700/epoxy UD CFRP (primary spar + carry-through); secondary bodies team-selected (CFRP sandwich / Al 6061 fittings)

load_cases.name: pullup_+4g
load_cases.application: elliptical lift across span on wing main spar and ribs, quarter-chord

load_cases.name: pushover_-2g
load_cases.application: inverted elliptical lift on wing main spar and ribs

load_cases.name: asymmetric_roll
load_cases.application: unbalanced half-span lift through ribs into main spar

load_cases.name: hard_landing_3g
load_cases.application: main landing-gear struts into fuselage forward-shell frame, wings in 1g trim

load_cases.name: ground_gust
load_cases.application: drag at wingtip, reacted through outer rib and main spar to carry-through

parts.name: wing_main_spar
parts.role: primary bending member carrying full-span lift distribution and root carry-through transfer

parts.name: wing_rib
parts.role: spanwise-distributed airfoil-shape stations carrying lift into spar
parts.count_note: ~8 ribs typical for 3.2 m span at ~400 mm pitch

parts.name: wing_root_carry_through_block
parts.role: transfers root bending moment and shear from spar into fuselage frame via 4-bolt pattern
parts.material_hint: T700/epoxy UD CFRP or forged Al 7075

parts.name: fuselage_forward_shell
parts.role: houses payload bay, motor mount attachment, landing-gear strut mounts, and wing carry-through fittings
parts.material_hint: CFRP monocoque or Kevlar/CF hybrid sandwich

parts.name: fuselage_aft_shell
parts.role: transitions from forward shell to tail-boom clamp, houses avionics bay

parts.name: tail_boom
parts.role: cantilever tube carrying empennage loads back to fuselage aft shell clamp

parts.name: horizontal_stabilizer
parts.role: pitch control surface mounted to aft end of tail boom

parts.name: vertical_stabilizer
parts.role: yaw stability surface mounted above tail-boom aft end

parts.name: landing_gear_strut
parts.role: reacts 3 g hard-landing vertical load from runway into fuselage forward-shell frame
parts.count_note: left and right main landing-gear legs; nose or tail wheel handled by aft-shell reinforcement

parts.name: motor_mount_plate
parts.role: supports propulsion motor at fuselage forward-shell nose ring

parts.name: payload_hatch
parts.role: removable access cover over payload bay in fuselage forward shell; bolted closure

parts.name: carry_through_fastener_set
parts.role: 4 x M6 A4-70 stainless bolts fixing C to D at wing-fuselage joint; governs R5 bolt-bearing gate

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: wing 3.2 m span x 0.35 m chord; fuselage ~1.3 m nose-to-tail plus ~0.9 m tail boom
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: x along fuselage longitudinal axis (nose +), y spanwise (starboard +), z vertical (up +); origin at carry-through centreline on fuselage floor

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: applied load = 1.5 x limit per STANAG 4703

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces (IF1..IF10) <= 1.0 under governing load case (LC1 for wing path, LC4 for landing-gear path).

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: layered shell for A/B/C/D/E/G/H skins; solid TET10 for carry-through block C; beam for tail boom F and struts I; tied/coupled connectors at each IF
analysis_types: linear static (x5 at ultimate = 1.5 x limit)

interfaces.load_path: rib shear flowing from airfoil skin into main spar along bond line
root bending + shear from spar into carry-through block
carry-through block bolted through fuselage forward-shell frame via 4 x M6 pattern (L)
forward-to-aft shell flange joint, longitudinal shear and bending
tail-boom clamp carries empennage bending back into aft shell
horizontal stabilizer bolted to tail boom aft fitting
vertical stabilizer bolted to tail boom aft fitting
landing-gear strut bolted to fuselage forward-shell frame; reacts LC4
motor mount plate bolted to forward-shell nose ring
payload hatch bolted to payload-bay frame; non-primary load path
M6 fastener shank carries bolt-bearing stress (R5) across carry-through block and shell frame

fem_check_policy.rationale: Wing loads and landing-gear loads share the fuselage forward-shell frame; only the assembled airframe exposes the true stiffness of the C-D bolted joint and the tail-boom clamp. Per-part stress is reported for visibility but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Free-Mission UAV rule scope encompasses wing primary structure joined to a fuselage that must also accept landing-gear, motor, and empennage loads; modelling the airframe as one solid body would collapse the bolted carry-through and the tail-boom clamp that dominate the R5 and R3 gates.
notes.speculation_caveats: Rib count (8), strut count (2), and fastener set cardinality (4) are rulebook-consistent but secondary bodies (D, E, F, G, H, J, K) are speculative in geometry and material; team may substitute equivalents.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
